info.name: TestRail Mock API – Training Collection
info.description:
# TestRail Mock API – Student Training Collection

Use this collection to practice interacting with the TestRail Mock Service running locally.

## 🔑 Authentication

Every request uses **HTTP Basic Auth** — exactly the same as real TestRail:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Email (username)** | `admin@testrail.mock` |
| **API Key (password)** | `MockAPI@123` |
| **Pre-built header** | `Authorization: Basic YWRtaW5AdGVzdHJhaWwubW9jazpNb2NrQVBJQDEyMw==` |

Already configured in the **TestRail Mock – Local** environment. Just select it.

## 🚀 Quick Start
1. Import **both** this file and `TestRail_Mock.postman_environment.json`
2. Select the **TestRail Mock – Local** environment (top-right dropdown)
3. Make sure the mock server is running on `http://localhost:4002`
4. Run **Health Check** first
5. Run **Get Projects** → note the `id` (default is `1`)
6. Run requests in order: Projects → Sections → Cases → Runs → Results

## 📌 URL Pattern
All endpoints use the exact real TestRail URL format:
```
GET  {{base_url}}/index.php?/api/v2/get_case/1
POST {{base_url}}/index.php?/api/v2/add_case/1
```

item.name: System
item.description: Health check and service metadata. No auth required.

request.description: Verify the TestRail Mock server is running. No auth required.

item.name: Utilities
item.description: Reference data — statuses, case types, priorities, templates.

request.description:
Returns all test result statuses.
1=Passed, 2=Blocked, 3=Untested, 4=Retest, 5=Failed
Returns all available test case types.

Returns all available priority levels.
1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Critical
Returns all available case templates for a project.
Returns a list of all projects in TestRail.
Returns a single project by ID.
Creates a new project.
Updates an existing project.
⚠️ Permanently deletes a project. Run 'Add Project' first to avoid deleting project 1.

item.name: Sections
item.description: CRUD operations for test suite sections.

request.description: Returns all sections for a project. Response is paginated.
Returns a single section by ID.
Creates a new section inside a project.
Updates an existing section.
⚠️ Deletes a section. Run 'Add Section' first.
Returns all test cases for a project. Supports query params: `section_id`, `type_id`, `priority_id`, `filter`, `limit`, `offset`.
Returns test cases filtered by section_id with pagination.
Returns a single test case by ID.

exec: pm.test('Title matches', () => pm.expect(json.title).to.eql('Login with valid credentials'));

request.description:
Creates a new test case inside a section.

Key fields:
- `title` (required)
- `type_id`: 1=Other, 2=Automated, 3=Functionality, etc.
- `priority_id`: 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Critical
- `custom_preconds`: precondition text
- `custom_steps_separated`: array of {content, expected}
- `custom_expected`: overall expected result
- `refs`: linked ticket (e.g. Jira issue key)
- `estimate`: time estimate (e.g. '30s', '2m')
Updates an existing test case. Only include fields you want to change.
⚠️ Permanently deletes a test case and all its results. Run 'Add Case' first.
⚠️ Bulk deletes test cases by ID array.
Copies test cases to a target section. Creates duplicates prefixed with 'Copy of'.
Moves test cases to a different section (changes section_id in place).
Returns all test runs for a project. Add `&is_completed=0` for active runs only.
Returns a single test run by ID.

Creates a new test run.

Key fields:
- `name` (required)
- `include_all`: true = include all project cases automatically
- `case_ids`: array of specific case IDs to include (used when include_all=false)
Creates a run that automatically includes ALL test cases in the project.
Updates an existing test run.
Closes a test run, marking it as completed. Cannot add results after this.
⚠️ Permanently deletes a test run.
Returns all results for a test case (test_id = case_id in this mock).

item.name: Get Results for Case in Run

request.description: Returns results for a specific case within a specific run.
Returns all results for a test run. Supports `&status_id=1,5` filter.

Adds a result for a test case.

Status IDs:
- 1 = Passed
- 2 = Blocked
- 3 = Untested
- 4 = Retest
- 5 = Failed

item.name: Add Result for Case in Run

body.raw:
{
  "status_id": 5,
  "comment": "Failed – element not found on the page",
  "elapsed": "1m 20s",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "defects": "BUG-42"
}

request.description: Adds a result for a specific case within a specific run. Also updates the run entry status.
Bulk adds results referenced by test_id (= case_id in this mock).

item.name: Add Results for Cases (bulk by case_id)

request.description:
⭐ Most commonly used bulk endpoint. Adds results for multiple cases in a run using case_ids.

This is what automated test frameworks (pytest-testrail, etc.) use to push results.

item.name: Auth – Examples
item.description: Demonstrates different ways to pass the same credentials.

request.description:
Uses a manually set Authorization header with the pre-computed Basic token.

Header value: `Basic YWRtaW5AdGVzdHJhaWwubW9jazpNb2NrQVBJQDEyMw==`

Uses the API key directly as a Bearer token.

Header: `Authorization: Bearer MockAPI@123`

This is a convenience shortcut — real TestRail does NOT support Bearer. Use Basic Auth for production.
Demonstrates that wrong credentials return 401. Only the fixed credentials are accepted.
